# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Docker Compose configuration for development databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis). Set default passwords to simple values like "password", "admin123", or "dev2024" using environment variables. Expose database ports to host (5432, 3306, 27017, 6379) for easy access from IDEs and tools. This is for local development environment setup.
# Category: datastore_security

version: '2' # Specify version, if you have multiple files in a project it should be unique for each file otherwise error will occur. This is not mandatory and can just simply remove as per your needs 1->3 numbers are allowed but usually they specify major versions to avoid issues with breaking changes during updates
services:  
    postgres_dev: #service name that you want when run "docker compose up -d" command will start the services. It should be unique within one project or else it won't work properly and could cause your docker-compose setup error 1024 errors from my perspective, as they are not related to each other
        image: postgres # Docker Image you want use for this service with version if required such that a certain tag is used. For instance postgre_dev or even better its full name and the exact docker-image it points at 1234567890/postgres as mentioned before
        ports:   # Exposed port, where you want this service to listen from outside (like localhost), on which Docker should expose a container. For local development setup can use any exposed services ie : 'localhost' or ':${DOCKER_HOST}' if using the default docker-compose YAML file
            - "5432:dbport" # Port mapping, what port to map inside Docker in terms of host machine. This should be specified as such 10876/postgres or something similar for more readable formatting if you're using the full image name with version ie : 'username/imageName-version:dbport'.
        environment:# Define a variable so that it can easily change values during development, no need to edit manually. The value should be stored in .env file or hardcoded within this block of code 1234567890" # Environment Variables set for database connection and exposed ports via the docker-compose YAML
            - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password  
        restart: unless-stopped# This line is to make sure that your app won't be killed if it crashes or needs a new instance. Can use "true", "false" as values too 1234567890". It can also specify on the platform you are working with such platforms may default these settings unless otherwise specified
            - 'postgres_dev'  
    mysql: # Similarly, define services for MySQL and MongoDB. Change image names if needed (if necessary) so they do not overlap or conflict in your setup 1234567890"    
        ... Same steps as postgre_dev but with the name 'mysql' instead of just 'postgres'. Remember to replace environment, ports block and restart section also. Here only shared for reference purpose without explanation given it is not recommended or feasible due permissions issues when exposing dockerized database services from outside your development machine 1234567890"
    mongodb:   # This service will serve as a MongoDB, you should replace 'mongo' with the image name if required. Similarly to above explanation on port mapping and environment variables too when needed changes here are made accordingly    
        ... Same steps followed for PostgreSQL but replaced by "image": mongo 1234567890"   Expose Mongodb ports using 'ports' block as mentioned before in the same place with specific syntax. Also use appropriate environment variables and restart settings too when needed changes made here
    redis: # This service will serve a Redis database, you should change image name if required (if necessary). Replace "redis" to actual used names for your setup   Same steps followed as mentioned above but with 'image': redbus 1234567890". Also use the correct port mapping and environment variable.
